title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: This test plan targets the post-silicon validation of Socket RAPL functionality at the highest feature hierarchy, focusing on its integration and operation within the SOC. Socket RAPL provides power and frequency limit enforcement across multiple domains (core, memory, etc.), interfacing with BIOS, TPMI, and various IPs. Key flows include BIOS initialization and locking of RAPL limits, TPMI-based domain enumeration and access, propagation of power/frequency ceilings from root to leaf IMHs and CBB dies, aggregation and real-time reporting of performance status, and coordinated enforcement via cross-domain PID controllers. The plan covers interactions between BIOS, TPMI, IMHs, CBB dies, and system-level status reporting, ensuring robust enforcement and accurate system state reflection under all operational and stress scenarios.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: The scope encompasses validation of BIOS and TPMI configuration integrity (including programming, locking, and domain enumeration), propagation and enforcement of power/frequency limits across all IPs, aggregation and reporting of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS, stress and boundary testing (including negative scenarios and error handling), and cross-domain PID controller interactions. All flows from initialization to runtime enforcement, status reporting, and system response to invalid or extreme configurations are included. Out-of-scope items are any features not directly related to Socket RAPL, such as non-RAPL power management, unrelated BIOS/TPMI features, or IPs outside the RAPL domain.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: All features require access to BIOS configuration interfaces, TPMI registers, CSR access for RAPL limits and lock bits, and system-level monitoring tools for status registers. Hardware must support domain enumeration and real-time status reporting. Stress tools for generating extreme power/frequency demands and error injection capabilities are needed.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Potential security threats include unauthorized modification of RAPL limits post-lock, improper domain enumeration exposing sensitive configuration, and manipulation of status reporting. Mitigations involve strict enforcement of lock bits, access control on TPMI and CSR interfaces, and validation of error handling for invalid configuration attempts.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of cross-domain interactions, undetected failures in lock enforcement, and inaccurate status aggregation under stress. Contingencies involve additional targeted testing for edge cases, enhanced monitoring during stress scenarios, and fallback to manual validation if automated tools fail.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Prerequisites include a fully functional BIOS with RAPL programming capability, TPMI register access, SOC hardware supporting all RAPL domains, and availability of stress and error injection tools. Initial domain mapping and lock bit configuration must be completed prior to test execution.

title: BIOS & TPMI Configuration Integrity
subsection content: Validate BIOS programming of Socket RAPL limits and time windows via CSR and TPMI, including lock bit enforcement. Test triggers for register modification attempts post-lock, domain mapping configuration, and verify that TPMI enumerates domains/features correctly. Expected outcome: limits are initialized, locked, and cannot be overridden at runtime.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Validate BIOS programming and lock enforcement of PACKAGE_POWER_SKU_CFG and PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT in CSR, and SOCKET_RAPL_PL_INFO, SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL, SOCKET_RAPL_PL2_CONTROL in TPMI, ensuring locked fields cannot be modified by in-band or out-of-band agents until reset.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: BIOS programs legal values for power limits and time windows, sets lock bits, and attempts are made to modify locked fields from in-band and out-of-band agents. Readbacks confirm lock enforcement.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: No explicit randomization; legal values for PL1 and PL2 limits and time windows are selected within specified ranges.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Requires BIOS access to CSR and TPMI interfaces, platform-specific TDP and power limits, and legal value ranges for all registers.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Coverage includes all relevant fields in CSR and TPMI, both in-band and out-of-band access, and lock enforcement for all fields.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Involves BIOS, CSR, TPMI, in-band and out-of-band agents, and lock bit enforcement across both interfaces.

title: CSR and TPMI Socket RAPL Limit Initialization and Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Requires observability of all fields post-lock, and validation that only a system reset clears the lock.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Program PACKAGE_POWER_SKU_CFG and PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT registers in CSR with platform-specific TDP, power limits, and time windows within legal ranges. Set the LOCK bit in PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT. After lock, attempt to write new values to any locked fields from both in-band and out-of-band agents. Read back all fields to confirm lock enforcement.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: 1. Boot system and ensure BIOS access to CSR. 2. Program PACKAGE_POWER_SKU_CFG and PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT with legal values for TDP, PL1, PL2, and time windows. 3. Set the LOCK bit in PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT. 4. Attempt to write new values to locked fields from in-band (OS/driver) and out-of-band (BMC/PECI) agents. 5. Read back all fields from CSR. 6. Reset system and verify lock is cleared.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Pass if all locked fields remain unchanged after attempted writes and only a system reset clears the lock. Fail if any field is modified post-lock or lock is cleared without reset.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks and logs that locked fields are not modified and lock is only cleared by reset.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for lock bit and register access.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS as per platform, PMON latest version, PythonSV for register access, Supercollider for concurrency, Perspec path for scenario automation.

title: CSR Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support and lock programming, OS kernel with register access utilities, PCIe/storage drivers as per platform, no special registry keys required.

After lock, attempt to write new values to any locked fields from both in-band and out-of-band agents. Read back all fields to confirm lock enforcement.

Attempt to write new values to locked fields from in-band and out-of-band agents. 5. Read back all fields from TPMI. 6. Reset system and verify lock is cleared.

title: TPMI Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Pass if all locked fields remain unchanged after attempted writes and only a system reset clears the lock. Fail if any field is modified post-lock or lock is cleared without reset.

title: TPMI Register Lock Enforcement
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks and logs that locked fields are not modified and lock is only cleared by reset.

subsection content: After programming and locking both CSR and TPMI registers, attempt to write to locked fields from all possible agents. Read back all fields to confirm no modification. Perform a system reset and verify that lock bits are cleared and registers can be reprogrammed.

Read back all fields to confirm no change. 4. Perform a system reset. 5. Read back registers to confirm lock bits are cleared and fields are writable.

title: Cross-Interface Lock Enforcement and Reset Behavior
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks and logs before and after reset.

subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for lock bit and register access.

title: Randomized RAPL Limit and Time Window Programming with Clipping Verification
subsection content: Validate BIOS programming of random legal and out-of-range values for PL1 and PL2 power limits and time windows in CSR and TPMI, and verify that out-of-range values are clipped to legal maximum/minimum.

title: Randomized RAPL Limit and Time Window Programming with Clipping Verification
subsection content: Randomly select values for PL1 and PL2 limits and time windows, program them into registers, set lock bits, and read back values to verify legal range and clipping.

Program these values into PACKAGE_RAPL_LIMIT (CSR) and SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL/SOCKET_RAPL_PL2_CONTROL (TPMI). Set the LOCK bits. Read back the programmed values to confirm they match the input.

title: Cross-Interface Clipping Consistency
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with RAPL support, OS kernel with register access utilities.

title: Cross-Interface Clipping Consistency
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with CSR and TPMI interfaces, correct SoC stepping, debug instrumentation for register access.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Validate that PL1 is always enforced in TPMI SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL regardless of the value of PWR_LIM_EN, and that attempts to disable PL1 are ignored.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: BIOS programs various values for PWR_LIM_EN, including disabling it, sets LOCK, and attempts to disable PL1 via TPMI. Power limiting and status are monitored to confirm PL1 is always enforced.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Various values for PWR_LIM_EN are programmed, including 0 (disabled) and 1 (enabled).

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Requires BIOS access to TPMI SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL and monitoring of power limiting behavior.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Coverage includes all possible values of PWR_LIM_EN and monitoring of PL1 enforcement.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Involves BIOS, TPMI, and Primecode enforcement logic for PL1.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Requires observability of enable status and power limiting behavior after programming PWR_LIM_EN.

title: TPMI PL1 Always Enabled and EN Bit Ignored
subsection content: Attempt to disable PL1 and verify it remains enabled.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Program TPMI SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN set to 0 (disabled). Set the LOCK bit. Attempt to disable PL1 via TPMI and monitor power limiting and status. Read back the enable status and confirm that PL1 remains enforced.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: 1. Boot system and ensure BIOS access to TPMI. 2. Program SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN = 0. 3. Set the LOCK bit. 4. Attempt to disable PL1 via TPMI. 5. Monitor power limiting and status. 6. Read back enable status. 7. Log all results.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Pass if PL1 remains enforced and power limiting is active regardless of PWR_LIM_EN value. Fail if PL1 is disabled or power limiting is not enforced.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks and power monitoring that PL1 is always enforced.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for PL1 enforcement.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS as per platform, PMON latest version, PythonSV for TPMI access, Supercollider for concurrency.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with TPMI RAPL support, OS kernel with TPMI access utilities.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Ignored Verification
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with TPMI interface, correct SoC stepping, debug instrumentation for TPMI access.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Program TPMI SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN toggled between 0 and 1. Set the LOCK bit after each programming. Attempt to disable and enable PL1 via TPMI and monitor power limiting and status. Read back enable status and confirm that PL1 remains enforced in all cases.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: 1. Boot system and ensure BIOS access to TPMI. 2. Program SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN = 0. 3. Set the LOCK bit. 4. Attempt to disable PL1 via TPMI. 5. Monitor power limiting and status. 6. Program SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN = 1. 7. Set the LOCK bit. 8. Attempt to enable PL1 via TPMI. 9. Monitor power limiting and status. 10. Read back enable status. 11. Log all results.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Pass if PL1 remains enforced and power limiting is active regardless of PWR_LIM_EN value. Fail if PL1 is disabled or power limiting is not enforced.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks and power monitoring that PL1 is always enforced.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for PL1 enforcement.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS as per platform, PMON latest version, PythonSV for TPMI access, Supercollider for concurrency.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with TPMI RAPL support, OS kernel with TPMI access utilities.

title: PL1 Enable Bit Toggle Test
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with TPMI interface, correct SoC stepping, debug instrumentation for TPMI access.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Program TPMI SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN = 0 and set the LOCK bit. Run a synthetic workload to stress the CPU and monitor power limiting and status to confirm that PL1 is enforced and power limiting is active.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: 1. Boot system and ensure BIOS access to TPMI. 2. Program SOCKET_RAPL_PL1_CONTROL with PWR_LIM_EN = 0. 3. Set the LOCK bit. 4. Run a synthetic workload to stress the CPU. 5. Monitor power limiting and status during workload. 6. Read back enable status. 7. Log all results.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Pass if PL1 is enforced and power limiting is active during stress regardless of PWR_LIM_EN value. Fail if PL1 is disabled or power limiting is not enforced.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Confirm via register readbacks, power monitoring, and workload logs that PL1 is always enforced.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for PL1 enforcement.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS as per platform, PMON latest version, PythonSV for TPMI access, Supercollider for concurrency, synthetic workload generator.

title: PL1 Enforcement Under Stress
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS with TPMI RAPL support, OS kernel with TPMI access utilities.

subsection content: Platform with TPMI interface, correct SoC stepping, debug instrumentation for TPMI access.
